merger_notes: PR #367 (billwatch 50-state SEO pages WIP for #333) and PR #368 (afterloss phone script SEO pages WIP for #292) merged. Both WIP PRs with solid work — no bugs, just unfinished features. Zero open PRs remain.

selected_issues.title: [billwatch] Provider database with deregulated market data and energy switching CTA
selected_issues.reason: P0 REVENUE — CONTINUATION (3rd cycle). Closest issue to direct revenue in the entire backlog. Energy switching CTAs for 18+ deregulated states monetize immediately via referrals. WIP PR #359 merged cycle 28 with partial provider DB. Worker should inspect existing provider-related files in billwatch/src/ and COMPLETE: provider database seeded with real deregulated market data, provider comparison UI, and energy switching CTAs with affiliate links. BillWatch has 76 PRs, mature codebase. This is the #1 revenue priority.

selected_issues.title: [billwatch] 50-state "Why is my electric bill so high in [state]" programmatic SEO pages
selected_issues.reason: P1 SEO TRAFFIC — NEW. 51 indexed pages targeting the highest-volume search query in BillWatch's domain. Each state page drives organic traffic to BillWatch. Well-scoped programmatic SEO: template + state data + build pipeline. No dependencies. Pairs with #334 — one builds the conversion pages (provider switching), the other builds the traffic funnel (state SEO pages). Together they create a complete revenue loop: traffic → content → energy switching CTA → revenue.

selected_issues.title: [afterloss] Phone script SEO landing pages — /phone-scripts/{service} with HowTo schema markup
selected_issues.reason: P1 SEO QUICK WIN — NEW, DIFFERENT PRODUCT. Phone script pages are standalone content with HowTo schema markup, targeting long-tail searches like 'what to say when canceling [service] for deceased'. No dependencies, well-scoped template work. AfterLoss has 150+ state pages (#258 completed) but needs more content surface area. These pages can embed affiliate links directly without the checklist engine (#254). Quick win with high completion probability — breaks the pattern of re-selecting stalling continuation issues.

housekeeping.reason: ORCHESTRATE CYCLE 31 (2026-03-19 10:30 UTC): STRATEGY PIVOT — Dropping 2 of 3 continuation issues that ran 3+ cycles without completing. KEPT #334 (billwatch provider DB, direct revenue). DROPPED #304 (afterloss click tracking — analytics infrastructure with nothing to track, since #302 affiliate links blocked by #254). DROPPED #345 (cellscore FCC data — infrastructure far from revenue). ADDED #333 (billwatch 50-state SEO) and #292 (afterloss phone script SEO) — both are well-scoped content issues with zero deps and high completion probability. Revenue thesis: #334 builds the conversion mechanism (energy switching CTAs), #333 drives traffic to it, #292 expands afterloss SEO footprint. PORTFOLIO: 2 billwatch + 1 afterloss. BillWatch gets 2 workers because it has the clearest path to first dollar (energy switching affiliate in deregulated markets).
